title: tanacetopsis eriobasis subsp. tricholepis
benefits: Tanacetopsis eriobasis subsp. tricholepis has many benefits, such as its use in traditional medicine to alleviate various health issues, including fever reduction, digestive problems, and respiratory issues. This plant's extracts have also been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to improve wound healing, reduce the risk of infections, and protect against cell damage. Furthermore, the plant has been traditionally used to treat various conditions, including headaches, rheumatism, and skin conditions, improving overall quality of life and reducing the reliance on pharmaceuticals. By harnessing the benefits of Tanacetopsis eriobasis subsp. tricholepis, individuals can enjoy improved health, increased energy levels, and enhanced well-being, ultimately leading to a more fulfilling and productive life.
actions: Tanacetopsis eriobasis subsp. tricholepis has many therapeutic actions, such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential in treating various health conditions. The plant's therapeutic actions may improve people's daily lives by reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation and better sleep, and protecting against cell damage and oxidative stress. Additionally, its antifungal and antibacterial properties can help prevent infections and promote wound healing, while its antiseptic properties can aid in the management of skin conditions. By harnessing the therapeutic potential of Tanacetopsis eriobasis subsp. tricholepis, individuals can enjoy improved overall well-being, enhanced immune function, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases.
constituents: Tanacetopsis eriobasis subsp. tricholepis has many active constituents, such as sesquiterpenoids like erioballoyl, eriobalsin, and eriobasdiol, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reducing oxidative stress, and protecting against cell damage. Additionally, the plant contains flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities, preventing infections and promoting wound healing. The presence of saponins and phenolic acids in Tanacetopsis eriobasis subsp. tricholepis has also been reported, with saponins contributing to its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, and phenolic acids exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. By harnessing these medicinal constituents, researchers aim to develop novel treatments for various diseases, ultimately improving human health and quality of life.
side_effects: Tanacetopsis eriobasis subsp. tricholepis can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, which can be severe and even lead to dehydration. In rare cases, excessive consumption of the plant may cause allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Long-term use or high doses of Tanacetopsis eriobasis subsp. tricholepis may also lead to liver damage and kidney problems. Additionally, its use during pregnancy or breastfeeding can have adverse effects on the fetus or baby, making it essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using the plant.
